name: Sankaradeva
dates_notes: Late fifteenth- to sixteenth-century Assamese religious author and founder of the Ekaśaraṇa-nāma-dharma tradition.
core_thesis: Śaṅkaradeva is the Assamese founder of the Ekaśaraṇa-nāma-dharma tradition. For this roster he qualifies through theological and philosophical works rather than through devotional performance literature. The relevant attested work is the *Bhaktiratnākara*, a Sanskrit treatise with autocommentarial material on devotion and exclusive refuge in Kṛṣṇa. The entry therefore records him as a regional theological author whose argued work belongs to Vaiṣṇava bhakti Vedānta-adjacent thought, while excluding dramas and devotional song materials from the engaged-work list.

engaged_works.title: Bhaktiratnakara
engaged_works.ascription_notes: The gap report identifies the Sanskrit *Bhaktiratnākara* with autocommentary as Śaṅkaradeva's qualifying argued theological work.
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit theological treatise associated with Śaṅkaradeva's Ekaśaraṇa tradition. It treats devotion and single-minded refuge in Kṛṣṇa in an argued form, making it the appropriate work for this philosophical roster. Devotional songs, dramas, and performance texts are intentionally excluded here. No clean local text has been acquired.
